assumptions: Three lifestyle tiers per capital (a prestige-district home, a city-centre 1-bed, or a suburban 1-bed) with sourced average rents. Investment income is taxed, then rent + utilities come out; whatever is left buys EUR 100 restaurant dinners (per week) and EUR 2,000 one-week holidays (per year). Trading down a tier frees rent for travel.

sources: https://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/in/Tallinn (2026: 1-bed city centre EUR 716, outside centre EUR 523; Basic Utilities 85m2 = EUR 285/mo; 3-course meal for 2 = EUR 80)
https://kadriorgapartments.ee + city24.ee/kv.ee (Kadriorg/Old Town prestige 60-90 m2 EUR 800-1700, point 1300)
https://www.emta.ee/en/private-client + EY (Estonia flat personal income tax incl. investment income = 22% from 1 Jan 2025)
https://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/in/Riga (2026: 1-bed city centre EUR 528, outside centre EUR 355; Basic Utilities 85m2 = EUR 319/mo; 3-course meal for 2 = EUR 60)
https://www.ss.com / city24.lv (Klusais centrs prestige Art Nouveau rentals, point 1200)
https://taxsummaries.pwc.com/latvia (capital income standard 25.5% from 2025; transitional 20% on pre-2025 assets used as representative resident rate)
https://www.numbeo.com/cost-of-living/in/Vilnius (2026: 1-bed city centre EUR 766, outside centre EUR 567; Basic Utilities 85m2 = EUR 199/mo; 3-course meal for 2 = EUR 65)
https://www.domoplius.lt / investropa.com (Senamiestis/Old Town prestige 57-99 m2 EUR 1200-1500, point 1350)
https://taxsummaries.pwc.com/lithuania (GPM on dividends/representative capital gains = flat 15%)
